# hallmarks (h) only. Their verbs: rekon (member-list), audit
  # (catalog-list), wrest (pull), Jettison (delete).
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_IMAGE      "rbw-i"   "Image — Container image operations"
  z_mod="rbfl0_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_IMAGES            "rbw-il"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_list"                  "param1"  "List GAR contents by raw path (type-blind; omit arg for top namespaces)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_IMAGE            "rbw-iw"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 "param1"  "Wrest any image from registry by raw ref (path-polymorphic)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_IMAGE         "rbw-iJ"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"             "param1"  "Jettison any image tag/version from registry by raw ref (path-polymorphic)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_HALLMARK         "rbw-irh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_hallmark"        "param1"  "List ark basenames present under a hallmark's GAR subtree"
  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS        "rbw-iah" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_hallmarks"       ""  "Audit hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE   "rbw-iwh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 "param1"  "Wrest a hallmark image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE  "rbw-iJh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            "param1"  "Jettison a hallmark image tag from registry"

  # Marshal — lifecycle (rbw-M)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_MARSHAL    "rbw-M"   "Marshal — Lifecycle"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_ZERO           "rbw-MZ"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_zero"      ""  "Zero regime to blank template"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_PROOF          "rbw-MP"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_proof"     ""  "Proof repo for release testing"

  # Nameplate — cross-nameplate operations (rbw-n)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_NAMEPLATE  "rbw-n"   "Nameplate — Cross-nameplate operations"
  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_NAMEPLATES        "rbw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_list"    ""  "List all nameplates"
  buz_enroll RBZ_SURVEY_NAMEPLATES      "rbw-ni"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_survey"  ""  "Survey nameplate status"
  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_NAMEPLATES       "rbw-nv"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_audit"   ""  "Validate all nameplates"

  # Regime — config files (rbw-r)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_REGIME     "rbw-r"   "Regime — Config files"
  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE       "rbw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_render"    "param1"  "Render nameplate regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE     "rbw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_validate"  "param1"  "Validate nameplate regime"
  z_mod="rbrv_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_VESSELS           "rbw-rvl" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_list"      ""        "List vessel regimes"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL          "rbw-rvr" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_render"    "param1"  "Render vessel regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL        "rbw-rvv" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_validate"  "param1"  "Validate vessel regime"
  z_mod="rbfl0_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY         "rbw-rvy" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_yoke"      "param1"  "Yoke a reliquary touchmark into every vessel's rbrv.env (wildcard fan-out)"
  z_mod="rbrr_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_REPO            "rbw-rrr" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_render"    ""  "Render repo regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO          "rbw-rrv" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_validate"  ""  "Validate repo regime"
  z_mod="rbrd_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_DEPOT           "rbw-rdr" "${z_mod}" "rbrd_render"    ""        "Render depot regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_DEPOT         "rbw-rdv" "${z_mod}" "rbrd_validate"  ""        "Validate depot regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_DEPOT         "rbw-rdi" "${z_mod}" "rbrd_inscribe"  "param1"  "Inscribe RBRD tripwire image to GAR (bearer token via positional)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CHECK_DEPOT            "rbw-rdc" "${z_mod}" "rbrd_check"     "param1"  "Check local rbrd.env against inscribed tripwire (bearer token via positional)"
  z_mod="rbrp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR           "rbw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_render"    ""  "Render payor regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR         "rbw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_validate"  ""  "Validate payor regime"
  z_mod="rbro_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH           "rbw-ror" "${z_mod}" "rbro_render"    ""  "Render OAuth regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH         "rbw-rov" "${z_mod}" "rbro_validate"  ""  "Validate OAuth regime"
  z_mod="rbrs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_STATION         "rbw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_render"    ""  "Render station regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_STATION       "rbw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_validate"  ""  "Validate station regime"
  z_mod="rbra_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_AUTH            "rbw-rar" "${z_mod}" "rbra_render"    "param1"  "Render auth regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH          "rbw-rav" "${z_mod}" "rbra_validate"  "param1"  "Validate auth regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_AUTH              "rbw-ral" "${z_mod}" "rbra_list"      ""        "List auth regimes"

  # Theurge — test infrastructure (rbw-t). One pipeline: the theurge engine
  # (build/test/run/suite/single) dispatches through rbw_workbench like every
  # other rbw command — no second workbench. Suites fold the former
  # tP/tS/tT into rbw-ts imprints (gauntlet/skirmish/siege).
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_THEURGE    "rbw-t"   "Theurge — Test infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbtd/rbte_cli.sh"
  # Build/Test carry the imprint channel solely to forward cargo passthrough
  # args ("$@"); the 2-segment filename leaves BURD_TOKEN_3 empty, so no folio
  # is consumed. (Empty channel would drop the args; param1 would eat the first.)
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_BUILD    "rbw-tb"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_build"   "imprint"  "Build the theurge crate"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_TEST     "rbw-tt"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_test"    "imprint"  "Run theurge unit tests"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_SUITE    "rbw-ts"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_suite"   "imprint"  "Run a named test suite (fast/service/crucible/complete/gauntlet/skirmish/dogfight/siege/blockade)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_FIXTURE  "rbw-tf"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_run"     "param1"   "Run a single named test fixture"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_CASE     "rbw-tc"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_single"  "param1"   "Run one case against a charged crucible (omit to list fixtures/cases)"

  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_THEURGE    "rbw-t"   "Theurge — Test infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbtd/rbte_cli.sh"
  # Build/Test carry the imprint channel solely to forward cargo passthrough
  # args ("$@"); the 2-segment filename leaves BURD_TOKEN_3 empty, so no folio
  # is consumed. (Empty channel would drop the args; param1 would eat the first.)
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_BUILD    "rbw-tb"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_build"   "imprint"  "Build the theurge crate"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_TEST     "rbw-tt"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_test"    "imprint"  "Run theurge unit tests"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_SUITE    "rbw-ts"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_suite"   "imprint"  "Run a named test suite (fast/service/crucible/complete/gauntlet/skirmish/dogfight/siege/blockade)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_FIXTURE  "rbw-tf"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_run"     "param1"   "Run a single named test fixture"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_CASE     "rbw-tc"  "${z_mod}" "rbte_single"  "param1"   "Run one case against a charged crucible (omit to list fixtures/cases)"

questions.question: Which verb names the hallmark installer (reads the chained hallmark fact, writes the vessel's graft-image slot)? Colophon follows the letter: rbw-rv<x> beside yoke's rbw-rvy.

options.label: anoint (Recommended)
options.description: Ecclesiastical — squarely in the ordain/conjure/vouch/consecrate register; designating the chosen image onto the vessel. Grep-clean. Colophon rbw-rva, file rbfla_anoint.sh.

options.label: bequeath
options.description: Inheritance handed down from the previous dispatch — semantically exact for the previous-dir baton, but long and outside the existing asterism. Colophon rbw-rvb.

options.label: betroth
options.description: Joins vessel to image — distinctive and rare, but opens a romantic register the project doesn't currently use. Colophon rbw-rvb.

options.label: endow
options.description: Civic/institutional granting of property to the vessel. Grep-clean, modest. Colophon rbw-rve.

questions.question: How does the rewired onboarding graft case source its hallmark (the docket's 'Open at mount')?

options.label: Dynamic chain (Recommended)
options.description: Case runs kludge of a small conjure vessel → installer → ordain graft-demo. Fully in-universe (the heat's cinch), tracked-regime churn per run — the yoke precedent already living in this fixture's case 1. Cost: one local docker build inside the case.

options.label: Staged static
options.description: Prove the installer against a staged vessel copy with a hand-primed previous-dir fact. Cheaper per run, but fabricates the chain — in tension with the heat's in-universe-over-fabrication cinch.

912	== Word Selection
913	
914	Vocabulary is the primary interface of every system the
915	{mcm_editor}
916	choreographs:
917	names are how intent survives across minds and sessions,
918	and the specification machinery around them is scaffolding in their service.
919	A name that fails its reader fails the system,
920	however sound the code beneath it.
921	
922	Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
923	this section governs the right half — the word itself.
924	The rules below bind the word part of every
925	{mcm_quoin},
926	the display text of every
927	{mcm_linked_term},
928	and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
929	They constrain births, not the living —
930	existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.
931	
932	* *Semantic uniqueness.*
933	One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
934	A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
935	* *One word per concept.*
936	The dual of semantic uniqueness: no alias
937	{mcm_quoin_s} —
938	a concept already named is closed to a second word.
939	Near neighbors must be spaced:
940	adjacent definitions state their distinction, or one of the pair dies.
941	* *Vocabulary isolation.*
942	Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
943	A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
944	{mcm_trodden_word}
945	and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
946	Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
947	word-level checks merely verify.
948	* *The grep gate.*
949	A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
950	a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
951	* *Rare but real.*
952	Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
953	Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
954	Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
955	* *Word husbandry.*
956	A word's commonness matches its operation's traffic:
957	daily surfaces earn short, plain, instantly-guessable words;
958	rare ceremonies must not spend them —
959	mint longer, rarer, more colorful words there,
960	conserving the small stock of plain English for the surfaces that need it.
961	Husbandry lowers the commonness ceiling, never the
962	{mcm_fair_faced}
963	floor: color may rise with rarity; opacity may not.
964	* *Sibling initials.*
965	Wherever a sibling set shares an abbreviation namespace —
966	colophon suffixes, quoin sub-letters, any single-character discriminator —
967	elect words whose first letters stay distinct,
968	so the abbreviation can remain the word's initial.
969	The verbs orbiting one critical noun are siblings from birth,
970	abbreviation namespace or not:
971	verb families acquire one eventually, and by then the words are minted.
972	An election-time check beside the grep gate;
973	the word-side twin of sub-letter monosemy.
974	* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
975	A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
976	a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
977	* *Audible register.*
978	Sibling subsystems ride
979	{mcm_asterism_s}
980	whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
981	so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.
982	
983	[[mcm_asterism]]
984	{mcm_asterism}::
985	A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
986	The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
987	as a vocabulary family may span
988	{mcm_concept_model_s}.
989	Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
990	(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
991	so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
992	and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
993	for the
994	{mcm_digital_mind}
995	and the
996	{mcm_editor}
997	alike.
998	Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
999	Model-side rationale: `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
1000	prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.
1001	
1002	=== Exposure
1003	
1004	Word-selection care scales with who must read the name.
1005	Every persistent name sits on a ladder of increasing dressing:
1006	
1007	* {mcm_hearting} — interior names, uncatalogued, governed by prefix discipline alone.
1008	* A
1009	{mcm_quoin}
1010	— the catalogued default; the rules above bind.
1011	* {mcm_ashlar} — a
1012	{mcm_quoin}

1015	The ladder is deliberate at both ends:
1016	{mcm_hearting}
1017	is permission to leave interior names plain,

1019	Misclassification fails quiet in one direction —

1054	{mcm_hearting}
1055	is a deliberate grant of plainness, not neglect;

1057	{mcm_ashlar}

1059	
1060	[[mcm_fair_faced]]
1061	{mcm_fair_faced}::
1062	The property an
1063	{mcm_ashlar}
1064	must hold: finished so it needs no render coat.
1065	An operator meeting the word for the first time, mid-ceremony,
1066	must act correctly without a glossary lookup.
1067	Rare enough to land clean in the grep gate,
1068	common enough to guess warm —
1069	the deliberate tightening of rare-but-real at the wall's face.
1070	The
1071	{mcm_cold_probe}
1072	is its test.
1073	
1074	[[mcm_coffer]]
1075	{mcm_coffer}::
1076	The bounded budget of
1077	{mcm_ashlar_s}
1078	an operator can be asked to hold.
1079	Each mint spends from it;
1080	spec-grain precision collapses into fewer operator words wherever the distinction does not change what the operator does.
1081	The fine grain lives on in
1082	{mcm_quoin_s};
1083	the
1084	{mcm_coffer}
1085	governs only the wall's face.

1087	[[mcm_broadside]]

1089	The single public sheet where a project posts its
1090	{mcm_ashlar}
1091	vocabulary.

1093	presence on the
1094	{mcm_broadside}
1095	is the registry contract —
1096	an
1097	{mcm_ashlar}
1098	mint is incomplete until its entry exists.
1099	A project whose operators work through digital minds may mirror the sheet into agent context files;
1100	mirrors are derived, never canonical.
1101	
1102	[[mcm_cold_probe]]
1103	{mcm_cold_probe}::
1104	A
1105	{mcm_curation_action}
1106	testing

1115	
1116	[[mcm_antipattern]]
1117	{mcm_antipattern}::
1118	A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
1119	{mcm_curation_action_s}
1120	and reviews can cite it precisely.

1123

1126	An

1130	History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;

== Word Selection

Vocabulary is the primary interface of every system the
{mcm_editor}
choreographs:
names are how intent survives across minds and sessions,
and the specification machinery around them is scaffolding in their service.
A name that fails its reader fails the system,
however sound the code beneath it.

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.
The rules below bind the word part of every
{mcm_quoin},
the display text of every
{mcm_linked_term},
and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
They constrain births, not the living —
existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.

* *Semantic uniqueness.*
One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
* *One word per concept.*
The dual of semantic uniqueness: no alias
{mcm_quoin_s} —
a concept already named is closed to a second word.
Near neighbors must be spaced:
adjacent definitions state their distinction, or one of the pair dies.
* *Vocabulary isolation.*
Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
{mcm_trodden_word}
and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
word-level checks merely verify.
* *The grep gate.*
A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
* *Rare but real.*
Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
* *Word husbandry.*
A word's commonness matches its operation's traffic:
daily surfaces earn short, plain, instantly-guessable words;
rare ceremonies must not spend them —
mint longer, rarer, more colorful words there,
conserving the small stock of plain English for the surfaces that need it.
Husbandry lowers the commonness ceiling, never the
{mcm_fair_faced}
floor: color may rise with rarity; opacity may not.
* *Sibling initials.*
Wherever a sibling set shares an abbreviation namespace —
colophon suffixes, quoin sub-letters, any single-character discriminator —
elect words whose first letters stay distinct,
so the abbreviation can remain the word's initial.
The verbs orbiting one critical noun are siblings from birth,
abbreviation namespace or not:
verb families acquire one eventually, and by then the words are minted.
An election-time check beside the grep gate;
the word-side twin of sub-letter monosemy.
* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
* *Audible register.*
Sibling subsystems ride
{mcm_asterism_s}
whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.

[[mcm_asterism]]
{mcm_asterism}::
A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
as a vocabulary family may span
{mcm_concept_model_s}.
Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and the
{mcm_editor}
alike.
Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
Model-side rationale: `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.

=== Exposure

Word-selection care scales with who must read the name.
Every persistent name sits on a ladder of increasing dressing:

* {mcm_hearting} — interior names, uncatalogued, governed by prefix discipline alone.
* A
{mcm_quoin}
— the catalogued default; the rules above bind.
* {mcm_ashlar} — a
{mcm_quoin}
that faces the operator; everything below binds, plus the rules at its definition.

The ladder is deliberate at both ends:
{mcm_hearting}

Misclassification fails quiet in one direction —
a word that should be

{mcm_broadside}.

[[mcm_ashlar]]

Verbs name what the operator does or sees, never the mechanism beneath.
Words appearing in failure output are

[[mcm_hearting]]

A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).

a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —

The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
grep returns a thousand strangers.

{mcm_trodden_word}
fails retrieval for the

questions.question: What verb names the new command? (It takes the hallmark from the build you just ran and writes it into a vessel's graft-image slot — "<verb> the graft vessel with the last build's image.")

options.label: anoint (Recommended)
options.description: "Anoint the vessel" — ceremonially designate the chosen image. Strongest fit with the existing ordain / conjure / vouch / consecrate register. Colophon rbw-rva.

options.label: bequeath
options.description: "Bequeath the hallmark to the vessel" — the prior build hands its image down. Semantically exact for the previous-run chaining, slightly outside the register. Colophon rbw-rvb.

options.label: endow
options.description: "Endow the vessel with the image" — civic granting of property. Plain, modest, guessable. Colophon rbw-rve.

options.label: deed
options.description: "Deed the hallmark to the vessel" — short, concrete property transfer. But word husbandry says don't spend a short plain word on a rare ceremony. Colophon rbw-rvd.

questions.question: How should the rewired onboarding graft test get its hallmark to anoint?

options.label: Real chain (Recommended)
options.description: The case runs a real kludge of a small vessel, then anoints graft-demo with it, then ordains. Fully in-universe — matches the heat's cinch against fabricated test inputs — and the per-run churn of the committed rbrv.env is the same pattern yoke already established in this fixture. Cost: one local docker build inside the case.

options.label: Hand-primed fact
options.description: The case writes a fact file into the previous-dir itself and anoints from that (or anoints a staged untracked copy of the vessel). Cheaper per run, but the chain is fabricated — the same pattern this heat is elsewhere eliminating.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - kindle entry: the single rbfl inclusion-guard and
# kindle/sentinel, sourcing the Foundry Core entry (rbfck_) and the guard-free body
# clusters (rbfly_ yoke, rbfld_ delete, rbfln_ inventory, rbflw_ wrest). The readonly
# ZRBFL_* constants the kindle sets are read globally.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFL_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core entry and the guard-free body clusters
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_core.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfly_yoke.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfld_delete.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfln_inventory.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbflw_wrest.sh"

# Cross-source from the rbld (Lode) family: abjure dispatches its GAR package
# delete cloud-side through the build-assembly spine and the shared cloud-delete
# body, exactly as banish does (the cinch-blessed narrow cross into made-side
# delete). Both carry their own inclusion guards, so this single-source-per-
# process reach raises no double-source; the spine reads only rbfc-level state
# (zrbfc_sentinel), which this process kindles.
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rblds_spine.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldd_delete.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfl_*)

zrbfl_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'Define delete operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_delete_"

  readonly ZRBFL_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfl_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfl not kindled - call zrbfl_kindle first"
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"

zrbfl_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_layout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_depot.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_derived.sh"
  source "${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_oauth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbuh_http.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbge_rest.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rba_auth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfl0_ledger.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbndb_base.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbrd_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbuh_kindle
  zrbge_kindle
  zrba_kindle
  zrbfc_kindle
  zrbfl_kindle
  zrbndb_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfl_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger" zrbfl_furnish "$@"

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

266:  rbcc_emit_consts || buc_die "rbz_emit_consts: rbcc emit failed"
273:# the theurge build wrapper both call this; rbq's gate verifies via rbz_emit_consts.

# rbz_emit_consts() - Emit the complete generated RBTDGC_ consts file to stdout.
# The single producer of rbtdgc_consts.rs content, shared by the theurge build
# (rbz_generate_consts) and rbq's freshness gate so the two never diverge.
# Two sections under one banner: the colophon group (this module binds the RBK
# projection prefixes — const name is RBTDGC_ + the enroll varname with its
# RBZ_ prefix stripped) followed by the rbcc single-homed set. rbcc_emit_consts
# is reachable here because every caller (rbq, the build, rblm) sources rbcc
# alongside rbz.
rbz_emit_consts() {
  zrbz_sentinel
  printf '%s\n' "// Generated by the theurge build — rbz colophons + rbcc constants. Do not edit."
  printf '%s\n' "// Regenerate: tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  buz_emit_colophon_consts "RBTDGC_" "RBZ_" || buc_die "rbz_emit_consts: colophon emit failed"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  rbcc_emit_consts || buc_die "rbz_emit_consts: rbcc emit failed"
}

input.description: Read rbcc consts emitter and verb tinder block

content.content:
# rbcc_emit_consts() - Emit the RBCC-owned co-maintained constants as Rust
# string consts to stdout, one `pub const` line per name/value pair via the
# shared buz_emit_const primitive (BUK must be kindled). The single-homed set:
# moorings/vessels dirs, account labels, .env filenames, operation verbs, and
# container roles. Each Rust const is
# RBTDGC_ + the RBCC stem (RBCC_ prefix stripped) uppercased; the value is
# carried verbatim. Bash stays mixed-case (RBCC_moorings_dir); the generated
# Rust is SCREAMING (RBTDGC_MOORINGS_DIR) per Rust convention — that casing is
# the sole transform, applied mechanically, so there is no per-entry mapping
# and no drift. rbtd's lib.rs paths, the manifest account-label mirror, and the
# rbtdrk/rbtdrp .env consts all source these instead of hand-copying.
rbcc_emit_consts() {
  printf '%s\n' "// RBCC constants (rbcc_constants.sh single-homed set)"

  local z_name=""
  local z_stem=""
  local z_upper=""
  for z_name in \
    RBCC_moorings_dir    \
    RBCC_vessels_subdir  \
    RBCC_account_governor   \
    RBCC_account_retriever  \
    RBCC_account_director   \
    RBCC_account_payor      \
    RBCC_account_assay      \
    RBCC_account_mason      \
    RBCC_rbrr_file       \
    RBCC_rbrp_file       \
    RBCC_rbrm_file       \
    RBCC_rbrd_basename   \
    RBCC_rbrd_file       \
    RBCC_rbrs_file       \
    RBCC_rbrn_file       \
    RBCC_rbra_file       \
    RBCC_rbro_file       \
    RBCC_verb_divest     \
    RBCC_verb_invest     \
    RBCC_verb_roster     \
    RBCC_verb_inscribe   \
    RBCC_verb_kludge     \
    RBCC_verb_ordain     \
    RBCC_verb_yoke       \
    RBCC_container_bottle    \
    RBCC_container_pentacle  \
    RBCC_container_sentry    \
  ; do
    z_stem="${z_name#RBCC_}"
    z_upper="$(printf '%s' "${z_stem}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')"
    buz_emit_const "RBTDGC_${z_upper}" "${!z_name}" \
      || buc_die "rbcc_emit_consts: emit failed for ${z_name}"
  done
}
RBCC_account_payor="payor"
RBCC_account_assay="assay"
RBCC_account_mason="mason"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Operation-verb tinder — the canonical bash home for RBK operation verbs.
# Members are bare verb tokens; the group carries one author here so it is
# projectable under the single-canonical-author rule. Two surfaces:
#
#   SA-management (divest/invest/roster) — composed into the fact-extension
#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
#
#   Image/build lifecycle (inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.
RBCC_verb_inscribe="inscribe"
RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_director}"
RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"

# Container-role tinder — the canonical bash home for the crucible's container

#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
#
#   Image/build lifecycle (inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.
RBCC_verb_inscribe="inscribe"
RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_director}"
RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"

# Container-role tinder — the canonical bash home for the crucible's container

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - anoint cluster (guard-free, sourced by rbfl0_):
# read the previous dispatch's build facts and rewrite RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE in one
# graft vessel's rbrv.env. Operator-committed, never self-committing.

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Anoint (rbfl_*)

rbfl_anoint() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local -r z_vessel="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Anoint a graft vessel with the previous build's hallmark — read the chained build facts and rewrite RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE in the vessel's rbrv.env"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel required (param1)"

  # Resolve and load the vessel; anoint addresses graft-mode vessels only.
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${z_vessel}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "rbnve_graft" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a graft vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"

  # Read the chained build facts — the previous dispatch must be a build
  # (kludge or ordain); buf_read_fact fails hard on a broken chain.
  buc_step "Reading chained build facts"
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(buf_read_fact "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "No hallmark fact from the previous dispatch — run a build (kludge or ordain) immediately before anoint"
  local z_gar_root=""
  z_gar_root=$(buf_read_fact "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}") \
    || buc_die "No gar_root fact from the previous dispatch"
  local z_ark_stem=""
  z_ark_stem=$(buf_read_fact "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}") \
    || buc_die "No ark_stem fact from the previous dispatch"

  local -r z_image_ref="${z_gar_root}/${z_ark_stem}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Image:    ${z_image_ref}"

  # Graft pushes from the local docker cache; a cloud-built hallmark is not
  # local, so flag the gap now rather than letting graft refuse later.
  docker image inspect "${z_image_ref}" > /dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_warn "Image not in the local docker cache — graft will refuse until it is present locally"

  buc_step "Anointing ${RBRV_SIGIL}"

  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir%/}/rbrv.env"
  test -f "${z_rbrv_file}" || buc_die "Vessel regime file not found: ${z_rbrv_file}"

  local z_rbrv_lines=()
  local z_rbrv_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_rbrv_line || test -n "${z_rbrv_line}"; do
    z_rbrv_lines+=("${z_rbrv_line}")
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"

  local -r z_tmp_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_anoint_${RBRV_SIGIL}_rbrv.env.new"
  : > "${z_tmp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to create ${z_tmp_file}"

  local z_wrote=0
  local z_j=""
  for z_j in "${!z_rbrv_lines[@]}"; do
    case "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" in
      RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE=*)
        printf 'RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE=%s\n' "${z_image_ref}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
          || buc_die "Failed to write RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
        z_wrote=1
        ;;
      *)
        printf '%s\n' "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
          || buc_die "Failed to write line for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
        ;;
    esac
  done

  case "${z_wrote}" in
    1) ;;
    *) printf '\n# Grafting Configuration\nRBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE=%s\n' "${z_image_ref}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
         || buc_die "Failed to append RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE for ${RBRV_SIGIL}" ;;
  esac

  mv "${z_tmp_file}" "${z_rbrv_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to finalize ${z_rbrv_file}"

  buc_success "Anointed ${RBRV_SIGIL} with ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Commit the rbrv.env change with your usual git workflow."
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - kindle entry: the single rbfl inclusion-guard and
# kindle/sentinel, sourcing the Foundry Core entry (rbfck_) and the guard-free body
# clusters (rbfly_ yoke, rbfld_ delete, rbfln_ inventory, rbflw_ wrest). The readonly
# ZRBFL_* constants the kindle sets are read globally.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFL_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core entry and the guard-free body clusters
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_core.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfly_yoke.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfld_delete.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfln_inventory.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbflw_wrest.sh"

# Cross-source from the rbld (Lode) family: abjure dispatches its GAR package
# delete cloud-side through the build-assembly spine and the shared cloud-delete
# body, exactly as banish does (the cinch-blessed narrow cross into made-side

RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Operation-verb tinder — the canonical bash home for RBK operation verbs.
# Members are bare verb tokens; the group carries one author here so it is
# projectable under the single-canonical-author rule. Two surfaces:
#
#   SA-management (divest/invest/roster) — composed into the fact-extension
#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
#
#   Image/build lifecycle (inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.

RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - kindle entry: the single rbfl inclusion-guard and
# kindle/sentinel, sourcing the Foundry Core entry (rbfck_) and the guard-free body
# clusters (rbfly_ yoke, rbfld_ delete, rbfln_ inventory, rbflw_ wrest). The readonly
# ZRBFL_* constants the kindle sets are read globally.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFL_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core entry and the guard-free body clusters
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_core.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfly_yoke.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfld_delete.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfln_inventory.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbflw_wrest.sh"

# Cross-source from the rbld (Lode) family: abjure dispatches its GAR package
# delete cloud-side through the build-assembly spine and the shared cloud-delete
# body, exactly as banish does (the cinch-blessed narrow cross into made-side
# delete). Both carry their own inclusion guards, so this single-source-per-
# process reach raises no double-source; the spine reads only rbfc-level state
# (zrbfc_sentinel), which this process kindles.
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rblds_spine.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldd_delete.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfl_*)

zrbfl_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'Define delete operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_delete_"

  readonly ZRBFL_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfl_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfl not kindled - call zrbfl_kindle first"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Kit directory — source-time self-location. rbcc lives at the RBK kit root,
# so its own directory IS the kit dir. No launcher environment dependency;
# available the instant this file is sourced (BCG kit-self-location pattern,
# canon: rbtd/rbte_cli.sh).
readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Generated zipper-derived artifacts — the theurge build materializes both
# (write-on-change), rbq's gates verify them. Absolute, composed from the
# readonly kit dir (source-time, no kindle dependency).
readonly RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbtd/src/rbtdgc_consts.rs"
readonly RBCC_tabtarget_context_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md"

# ── Moorings inventory constants ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Moorings-relative path values. Every consumer reads these names directly
# (the transitional RBBC_* aliases were retired by the literal-sweep pace).
# Source-time literals, no kindle dependency.
RBCC_moorings_dir="rbmm_moorings"
RBCC_launchers_subdir="rbml_launchers"
RBCC_users_subdir="rbmu_users"
RBCC_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
RBCC_vessels_subdir="rbmv_vessels"
RBCC_rbrr_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrr.env"
RBCC_rbrp_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrp.env"
RBCC_rbrm_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrm.env"
RBCC_rbrd_basename="rbrd.env"
RBCC_rbrd_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_rbrd_basename}"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
# Auth-role enum (rbnae_): the RBRA_ROLE value — minted sprue, RBRA_ROLE domain
# (3 members). Written into credential files, validated, carried as the auth
# folio, swizzle-checked against BUZ_FOLIO. Distinct axis from RBCC_account_*
# below: that one is the bare composition label.
RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"

# Account composition labels — bare fragments that compose GCP SA account-ids/
# emails AND local secret-directory names (6 members, superset of the 3 roles
# plus payor/assay/mason which are NEVER RBRA_ROLE values). These stay bare:
# a derived resource-name string in cloud/filesystem space, structurally like
# the SA email and the -tether/-airgap pool suffixes the heat keeps bare.
RBCC_account_governor="governor"
RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_account_director="director"
RBCC_account_payor="payor"
RBCC_account_assay="assay"
RBCC_account_mason="mason"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Operation-verb tinder — the canonical bash home for RBK operation verbs.
# Members are bare verb tokens; the group carries one author here so it is
# projectable under the single-canonical-author rule. Two surfaces:
#
#   SA-management (divest/invest/roster) — composed into the fact-extension
#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
#
#   Image/build lifecycle (inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.
RBCC_verb_inscribe="inscribe"
RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_director}"
RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"

# Container-role tinder — the canonical bash home for the crucible's container
# roles. Bare role tokens; the crucible is sentry + pentacle + bottle and every
# container name / compose service derives from these. Distinct from the
# credential auth-role axis above, which itself splits in two: RBCC_role_*
# (minted enum, the RBRA_ROLE value) and RBCC_account_* (bare composition label
# for SA names + secret dirs). None of these words are reused across families,
# keeping each token monosemous.
RBCC_container_bottle="bottle"
RBCC_container_pentacle="pentacle"
RBCC_container_sentry="sentry"

######################################################################
# Rust const projection (rbcc single-homed set → RBTDGC_)

# rbcc_emit_consts() - Emit the RBCC-owned co-maintained constants as Rust
# string consts to stdout, one `pub const` line per name/value pair via the
# shared buz_emit_const primitive (BUK must be kindled). The single-homed set:
# moorings/vessels dirs, account labels, .env filenames, operation verbs, and
# container roles. Each Rust const is
# RBTDGC_ + the RBCC stem (RBCC_ prefix stripped) uppercased; the value is
# carried verbatim. Bash stays mixed-case (RBCC_moorings_dir); the generated
# Rust is SCREAMING (RBTDGC_MOORINGS_DIR) per Rust convention — that casing is
# the sole transform, applied mechanically, so there is no per-entry mapping
# and no drift. rbtd's lib.rs paths, the manifest account-label mirror, and the
# rbtdrk/rbtdrp .env consts all source these instead of hand-copying.
rbcc_emit_consts() {
  printf '%s\n' "// RBCC constants (rbcc_constants.sh single-homed set)"

  local z_name=""
  local z_stem=""
  local z_upper=""
  for z_name in \
    RBCC_moorings_dir    \
    RBCC_vessels_subdir  \
    RBCC_account_governor   \
    RBCC_account_retriever  \
    RBCC_account_director   \
    RBCC_account_payor      \
    RBCC_account_assay      \
    RBCC_account_mason      \
    RBCC_rbrr_file       \
    RBCC_rbrp_file       \
    RBCC_rbrm_file       \
    RBCC_rbrd_basename   \
    RBCC_rbrd_file       \
    RBCC_rbrs_file       \
    RBCC_rbrn_file       \
    RBCC_rbra_file       \
    RBCC_rbro_file       \
    RBCC_verb_divest     \
    RBCC_verb_invest     \
    RBCC_verb_roster     \
    RBCC_verb_inscribe   \
    RBCC_verb_kludge     \
    RBCC_verb_ordain     \
    RBCC_verb_yoke       \
    RBCC_container_bottle    \
    RBCC_container_pentacle  \
    RBCC_container_sentry    \
  ; do
    z_stem="${z_name#RBCC_}"
    z_upper="$(printf '%s' "${z_stem}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')"
    buz_emit_const "RBTDGC_${z_upper}" "${!z_name}" \
      || buc_die "rbcc_emit_consts: emit failed for ${z_name}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

RBCC_verb_divest     \

input.new_string:
RBCC_verb_divest     \

    RBCC_verb_anoint     \
    RBCC_verb_inscribe   \

RBCC_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
RBCC_vessels_subdir="rbmv_vessels"
RBCC_rbrr_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrr.env"
RBCC_rbrp_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrp.env"
RBCC_rbrm_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrm.env"
RBCC_rbrd_basename="rbrd.env"
RBCC_rbrd_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_rbrd_basename}"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
# Auth-role enum (rbnae_): the RBRA_ROLE value — minted sprue, RBRA_ROLE domain
# (3 members). Written into credential files, validated, carried as the auth
# folio, swizzle-checked against BUZ_FOLIO. Distinct axis from RBCC_account_*
# below: that one is the bare composition label.
RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"

# Account composition labels — bare fragments that compose GCP SA account-ids/
# emails AND local secret-directory names (6 members, superset of the 3 roles
# plus payor/assay/mason which are NEVER RBRA_ROLE values). These stay bare:
# a derived resource-name string in cloud/filesystem space, structurally like
# the SA email and the -tether/-airgap pool suffixes the heat keeps bare.
RBCC_account_governor="governor"
RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_account_director="director"
RBCC_account_payor="payor"
RBCC_account_assay="assay"
RBCC_account_mason="mason"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Operation-verb tinder — the canonical bash home for RBK operation verbs.
# Members are bare verb tokens; the group carries one author here so it is
# projectable under the single-canonical-author rule. Two surfaces:
#
#   SA-management (divest/invest/roster) — composed into the fact-extension
#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
#
#   Image/build lifecycle (anoint/inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.
RBCC_verb_anoint="anoint"
RBCC_verb_inscribe="inscribe"
RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_director}"
RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"

# Container-role tinder — the canonical bash home for the crucible's container
# roles. Bare role tokens; the crucible is sentry + pentacle + bottle and every
# container name / compose service derives from these. Distinct from the
# credential auth-role axis above, which itself splits in two: RBCC_role_*
# (minted enum, the RBRA_ROLE value) and RBCC_account_* (bare composition label
# for SA names + secret dirs). None of these words are reused across families,
# keeping each token monosemous.
RBCC_container_bottle="bottle"
RBCC_container_pentacle="pentacle"
RBCC_container_sentry="sentry"

######################################################################
# Rust const projection (rbcc single-homed set → RBTDGC_)

# rbcc_emit_consts() - Emit the RBCC-owned co-maintained constants as Rust
# string consts to stdout, one `pub const` line per name/value pair via the
# shared buz_emit_const primitive (BUK must be kindled). The single-homed set:
# moorings/vessels dirs, account labels, .env filenames, operation verbs, and
# container roles. Each Rust const is
# RBTDGC_ + the RBCC stem (RBCC_ prefix stripped) uppercased; the value is
# carried verbatim. Bash stays mixed-case (RBCC_moorings_dir); the generated
# Rust is SCREAMING (RBTDGC_MOORINGS_DIR) per Rust convention — that casing is
# the sole transform, applied mechanically, so there is no per-entry mapping
# and no drift. rbtd's lib.rs paths, the manifest account-label mirror, and the
# rbtdrk/rbtdrp .env consts all source these instead of hand-copying.
rbcc_emit_consts() {
  printf '%s\n' "// RBCC constants (rbcc_constants.sh single-homed set)"

  local z_name=""
  local z_stem=""
  local z_upper=""
  for z_name in \
    RBCC_moorings_dir    \
    RBCC_vessels_subdir  \
    RBCC_account_governor   \
    RBCC_account_retriever  \
    RBCC_account_director   \
    RBCC_account_payor      \
    RBCC_account_assay      \
    RBCC_account_mason      \
    RBCC_rbrr_file       \
    RBCC_rbrp_file       \
    RBCC_rbrm_file       \
    RBCC_rbrd_basename   \
    RBCC_rbrd_file       \
    RBCC_rbrs_file       \
    RBCC_rbrn_file       \
    RBCC_rbra_file       \
    RBCC_rbro_file       \
    RBCC_verb_divest     \
    RBCC_verb_invest     \
    RBCC_verb_roster     \
    RBCC_verb_inscribe   \
    RBCC_verb_kludge     \
    RBCC_verb_ordain     \
    RBCC_verb_yoke       \
    RBCC_container_bottle    \
    RBCC_container_pentacle  \
    RBCC_container_sentry    \
  ; do
    z_stem="${z_name#RBCC_}"
    z_upper="$(printf '%s' "${z_stem}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')"
    buz_emit_const "RBTDGC_${z_upper}" "${!z_name}" \
      || buc_die "rbcc_emit_consts: emit failed for ${z_name}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

# Load and validate vessel
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "rbnve_graft" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a graft vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"

  # Tweak override: test infrastructure injects the graft image via the bure tweak
  # channel. The name carries the buo sprue (BURE enforces the shape). Mirror:
  # rbtdro_onboarding.rs RBTDRO_GRAFT_TWEAK_NAME — same literal, cross-language.
  local -r z_graft_tweak_name="buorb_graft_image"
  test "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}" != "${z_graft_tweak_name}" || RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE}"

  test -n "${RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE not set for graft vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}'"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (graft about+vouch steps use tool images)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

# Grafting Configuration — four-mode fixture overrides via BURE_TWEAK; handbook
# usage either overrides similarly or documents replacing this line.
RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE=test_busybox:local-20251231-081227-44143-75

# eof

file.content:
######################################################################
# Sentinel

zrbte_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

# zrbte_codegen() - Refresh all zipper-derived artifacts before compiling.
# Write-on-change, so unchanged files keep their mtime and cargo skips rebuilds.
# The build is the sole producer — there is no standalone generate command.
zrbte_codegen() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  rbz_generate_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" "${RBCC_tabtarget_context_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate tabtarget context"
  rbz_generate_consts "${RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate colophon consts"
}

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST_ARG}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}
